content:
This perceived degree of "peace" comes at the cost of people having to tiptoe around some filter they most likely don't even know about.

This filter which, by the way, instantly deletes the message you sent - say for example you were discussing an important lore tidbit in <#1326453763355185215> but your paragraphs of yapping goes into the void because you mentioned the word "h\*ll" (when it has so many other alternative meanings!)

People don't know this filter exists, and the punishment for ignorance (a warn) is simply too much personally.

Is it not up to the moderation team to let people know beforehand what's allowed and what's not?

Why is there no communication between these actions and the community? 

This has already happened before by the way, in this very community https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1397433385500151888

||It certainly doesn't stop people from nagging at me about how I brick people's accounts or passive-agressively belittle me for "not helping the community" (which is js annoying at the very least).||

That being said, I hope there's more action from BIAV regarding server moderation, since it seems like you're the only one tbh. What this community needs on that regard is... more eyes to watch over what's been happening. 

The filter stops words, but not the animosity, and the latter does more damage than any slur can bring. I'm also sure people would be interested in volunteering to become a communuty moderator if given the chance.

Summary of my main concerns with using a swear filter.
1. Stopping people from swearing doesn't stop users with ill intent. It just forces people to become creative, and causes more moderation problems later on.
2. Words aren't the problem, it is specific people in any community who are being a negative influence. Instead of punishing the majority, if there's issues, work on people who are causing said problems.
3. This game is honestly at bare minimum 16+, and letting 13 year olds be in this server is a fundamental moderation issue. They shouldn't even *be* here, so a swear filter makes no sense, because it should be all adults in the first place.
4. A swear filter is no replacement for actual moderation. Context matters.

My biggest gripe with having a swear filter in the first place is the idea that you guys want to treat the average user here like a kindergartener.
It's a lot of work, and the more rules you put into place, especially unnecessary minor ones that impede both the users and the admin's ability to address problem users, the harder it is to moderate because more regular users will "act out" and you have to spend your time being stern and timing them out.
Do you want to spend your waking hours on this server enforcing unnecessary rules like a second job or do you want to let people know your expectations for the community and have them work on it together?
Yeah really it should just be targeted harassment from bad apples. If that's the case members can just ping mod
Or the harassed person themself
Again.. We understand the game is borderline rated R.  But like I said we are under Discord TOS. Plain and simple whether we like it or not🙏.  Thank you for understanding.
Discord ToS does not have a rule against cursing or swearing.
Could you please reference the part of Discord ToS that prohibits users from using words such as "аs$" and "h*ll"? <:yah:1419369149490004048>
You didn't address any of the criticism or points that were made against the implementation of the bot...or that there was no announcement...
There are better ways to handle this.
As the others said, there is nothing in discord TOS against swearing. This is nonsensical.

People will just skirt around the banned words which then defeats the whole point of the bot. And like what others have articulated better than I can, the animosity doesn't disappear at all. It's not the words that make the place unsafe and uncomfortable for people. It's the intent. You can ban as many words as you can, but it'll just be a bandaid that solves nothing.

https://discord.com/guidelines
Can you point out where in the Discord ToS the community was violating that necessitated these changes? And which ToS is this change supposed to enforce?
As an average gacha player, my reading ability isn't that great so it would be great if you could enlighten me.

Earlier there was a person straight up admitting to sexualizing children. Where was the automod? Did it help at all? No, because the guy didn't say any of the banned words and yet it undoubtedly made all of us uncomfortable.
implementing a chat filter that treats us as kindergardeners is offensive enough, but trying to directly treat us as kindergardeners is even more offensive
do you think we do not have the ability to read?
do you think we are dumb?
what is the goal of directly lying to our faces like this, I don't understand it
what discord tos are we talking about are my eyes deceiving me here

It was never the words that make people feel uncomfortable and unsafe. It's the context and tone and again, the intent. And what do we call a ban that aims to protect people and yet fails at it? We all know the answer here.

Isn't discord restricted to people 13 years or older anyway? I swore more at that age than I do now! I really don't think this solves any problems and the discord tos is a shoddy excuse given that this aggressive automoderation is not a requirement of it.

I double checked Discords hate speech policy, and the only thing explicitly prohibited is slurs that degrade or vilify based on protected characteristics. 

Believe it or not the word sh!t and many others that have been banned don't do that.

https://discord.com/safety/hateful-conduct-policy-explainer

Here's the explanation for it.

embeds.title: Hateful Conduct Policy Explainer | Discord
embeds.description: Read Discord’s Hateful Conduct Policy Explainer here.

content: Please clarify your statement 🙏 it makes it seem like you're ignoring other people's point

<#1354754985119846581>
Can we get Chinese version too?
fr

Hey, everyone, please don't get upset🙏 
We sincerely apologize for the fact that our simple ban on crude language has made you unhappy. Our intention was to prevent hurtful actions by reducing the use of such language. However, we acknowledge that we didn't consider everyone's language habits and emotional expression, which was a oversight on our part. We will adjust the scope of our banned word list, but we will still retain words with clear malicious intent, such as racial slurs.
Additionally, regarding our plan to involve more game administrators to help manage uncivil behavior in chat channels, we are actively working on it. We will announce updates as soon as we have more information.

We really appreciate the update and consideration. 
Another great addition, that would help the mod team in the future, is the implementation of a functioning Report System, if that would be possible.

I suggest making a channel for reporting.This channel message history hidden from regular members. Only the mod team can see messages sent here. This allows us to report issues without having to publicly ping or dm the moderators and its very easy to do
Are we going to get a Chinese version or does bot moderation only apply to English?

We will also add some very vulgar and filthy words in Chinese, please rest assured. We are not doing this to restrict everyone's speech, but in the process of setting up blocking words, we mistakenly added some commonly used words due to a lack of understanding of English language usage habits. The situation will improve by next Monday at the latest. Please trust us🌹
Oh I should be more clear. When I say Chinese version, I meant Chinese translations
Previous announcements all had Chinese translations
Okay, I'll add it on Monday🌹
milds profanity like the F and SH words shouldnt be banned
ily for that
